stories.title: Fix category heading i18n — add Romanian slug keys to content section
stories.description: ContentList.jsx uses t(`content.${category}`) but category is a Romanian slug (amenintari, ghid, educatie) while en.json content section has English keys (threats, guides, education). Fix: add Romanian slug aliases to the content section in ALL 5 i18n files. In en.json: add "amenintari": "Threats", "ghid": "Guides", "educatie": "Education", "povesti": "Real stories", "rapoarte": "Reports", "presa": "Press releases" alongside the existing English keys. Do the same for ro.json, bg.json, hu.json, uk.json — each file should have BOTH the English key AND the Romanian slug key mapping to the correct translation in that language. GitHub #412.

stories.title: Replace hardcoded Romanian labels in AlertDetail.jsx with i18n t() calls
stories.description: In src/ui/src/components/AlertDetail.jsx, replace all hardcoded Romanian strings with t() i18n calls. Strings to replace: 'Înapoi la alerte' → t('alert_detail.back'), 'Entitate vizată:' → t('alert_detail.target_entity'), 'Prima atestare:' → t('alert_detail.first_reported'), 'Vezi detalii complete' → t('alert_detail.view_details'), 'Alerta nu a fost găsită' → t('alert_detail.not_found'), 'Se încarcă...' → t('alert_detail.loading'). Add the alert_detail section to ALL 5 i18n files with proper translations: en.json (English), ro.json (Romanian), bg.json (Bulgarian), hu.json (Hungarian), uk.json (Ukrainian). For bg/hu/uk use reasonable translations or English fallback. GitHub #413.
